html:
Exclusive to:

PikoOrange Piko

The Piko is a neutral Mob exclusive to the Don't Starve: Hamlet DLC. There is also a rarer orange variant called the Orange Piko.

Both Piko and Orange Piko spawn from Tea Trees, and seem more likely to spawn near clumps of several trees growing close together.

Each Piko has a "home tea tree". They will only climb this tree, unless they are caught in a trap and released. They will retreat to their home tree at dusk, when frightened, or after picking up an item. They will not leave the tree until next dawn or the player relogs. They do not seem to spawn from tea trees planted by the player, unless one is caught in a trap and put on the ground near that tree. After a few seconds, the Piko will run up the nearest tree, "infesting" it with Piko. Chopping down an infested tree returns some of the items stolen by it's Piko. However, there is a bug which causes the infested tree's stump to still spawn and harbor Pikos. Digging up the stump solves this. Player-infested trees behave identically to naturally spawned ones.

If a Piko loses its home tree and has already picked up an item, it will seek out and eat Seeds Food on the ground. This is the only situation that will make Piko eat voluntarily, which may be a design error.

An infested tree always spawns the same color Piko each day, unless it's piko is killed or captured, in which case the color of the next piko is random.

If an item is dropped on the ground (either edible or not) near a Piko or Orange Piko, there is a chance that it will stock it in its cheeks and flee with it. It is common when you chop a Tea Tree that Pikos may spawn or race in from nearby trees and steal one of your Logs, Twigs, or Seed Pods. Take caution not to drop stacks of an item, as one Piko is able to steal it all.

Pikos will run from the player, unless they are trying to steal an item on the ground or there is a full moon/aporkalypse in progress.

During a Full Moon or Aporkalypse, Pikos will glow, their eyes turn red and they become aggressive. In addition, an attack from a Piko or Orange Piko will, like frogs, cause the item in the leftmost slot of the inventory bar to drop from the player's inventory. After said item is on the ground, the Piko/Orange Piko prioritizes storing the item in its cheeks and retreating to its home Tea Tree.

Pikos can be caught using a Trap. During a Full Moon, carrying a captured Piko will cause the player to emit a reddish glow, similar to the effects of eating a Glow Berry.

Piko will return to their home at dusk and on the night of the new moon. They leave their homes at the new moon and return immediately.

Usage

×2

×2

Tips

  • Be very careful when chopping Tea Trees at night with a Lantern on the ground. Pikos might steal it, and without any other light sources you will be completely vulnerable to Charlie.
  • Player can infest a tree with Pikos by releasing any captured piko nearby and letting it climb the tree.
  • Using an item to bait a Piko is an easy way to kill them, as they prioritize retrieving the item and running away with it rather than running from players when approached. Players can get free hits on Pikos as they run toward the item. If the Piko picked up the item, the player can retrieve it by killing the Piko, as they run toward a given Tea Tree in a predictable straight line.
    • Pikos cannot pick up backpacks, but will relentlessly try to anyways, making for very effective bait.
  • Interestingly, Abigail hates Pikos and will attack them if they are too near. An easy way to farm them for morsels.
  • Pikos can be used to obtain drops from pig guards without other nearby guards aggroing the player. If a piko picks up a guard drop then is killed it will drop what it picked up and the guards will no longer identify it as belonging to a guard and the player can pick it up without getting attacked.

Bugs

  • Pikos can hide in Tree stumps as if the tree was still present and will thus run up an invisible tree and disappear.
  • If dropped in a shop, game can crash.
  • if it tries to get a wet sunken object, you hitting it makes it walk on water. once it is on water, it will try to get back to its tea tree, but you have to hit it again for it to get back on land. you cannot use this to retrieve wet sunken objects as if you do it will stay as a wet sunken object, but on land.
  • If you kill a Piko just before it gets trapped it provides you with a morsel and a trapped Piko.
  • If you kill or capture an aggressive Piko on a full moon, another non-aggressive Piko will immediately spawn from it's tree.

Trivia

  • The use of Orange Pikos in making Tea and Iced Tea is a pun on orange pekoe tea.
  • The Piko may be loosely based off of the real-life pika, who are known to steal food from its own kind, and pica, an eating disorder characterized by the consumption of inedible items.

Gallery

  • A sleeping Piko.
  • A dead Piko.
  • A sleeping orange variant of the Piko.
  • A dead orange variant of the Piko.
  • On Concept Art from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • On Concept Art from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

PikoOrange Piko
“He's a bit of a nut job.”
–Wilson, examining a Piko
“Squirrelly little guy.”
–Willow, examining a Piko
“Tiny, weak fluff.”
–Wolfgang, examining a Piko
“Too naive to know pain.”
–Wendy, examining a Piko
“A NUT GATHERING MEAT MACHINE”
–WX-78, examining a Piko
“A charming mammalian fuzzball.”
–Wickerbottom, examining a Piko
“They like trees almost as much as us.”
–Woodie, examining a Piko
“Shoo, vermin.”
–Maxwell, examining a Piko
“The amazing storage space of their small cheeks defies all laws of physics!”
–Wagstaff, examining a Piko
“Döst thöu crave battle?”
–Wigfrid, examining a Piko
“He's a busy little guy.”
–Webber, examining a Piko
“It's a little buddy!”
–Walani, examining a Piko
“Bonjour, mon amie.”
–Warly, examining a Piko
“Thievin' scoundrel.”
–Woodlegs, examining a Piko
“FELLOWS OF INFINITE JEST”
–Wilba, examining a Piko
“Fluffy Nabber”
–Wormwood, examining a Piko
“Has more storage space in its cheeks than I do in my pockets.”
–Wheeler, examining a Piko
“For some reason I'm craving tea.”
–Wilson, examining an Orange Piko
“Squirrelly little guy.”
–Willow, examining an Orange Piko
“Tiny, weak orange fluff.”
–Wolfgang, examining an Orange Piko
“You exist to amuse others.”
–Wendy, examining an Orange Piko
“MEAT MACHINE WITH BREWING PROPERTIES”
–WX-78, examining an Orange Piko
“An amusingly named mammalian fuzzball.”
–Wickerbottom, examining an Orange Piko
“They like trees almost as much as us.”
–Woodie, examining an Orange Piko
“How amusing.”
–Maxwell, examining an Orange Piko
“Smells of tea. Perhaps a consequence of its proximity to tea trees?”
–Wagstaff, examining an Orange Piko
“I hath nö use för it.”
–Wigfrid, examining an Orange Piko
“I wonder if he likes tea parties.”
–Webber, examining an Orange Piko
“Oh, I get it.”
–Walani, examining an Orange Piko
“I prefer English Breakfast.”
–Warly, examining an Orange Piko
“Aye, 'tis a useful brewed.”
–Woodlegs, examining an Orange Piko
“OF MOST EXCELLENT FANCY TAILS”
–Wilba, examining an Orange Piko
“Fluffy Nabber”
–Wormwood, examining an Orange Piko
“Has a strangely caffeinated smell.”
–Wheeler, examining an Orange Piko

They will retreat to their home tree at dusk, when frightened, or after picking up an item. They will not leave the tree until next dawn or the player relogs. After a few seconds, the Piko will run up the nearest tree, "infesting" it with Piko. Chopping down an infested tree returns some of the items stolen by it's Piko. However, there is a bug which causes the infested tree's stump to still spawn and harbor Pikos. Digging up the stump solves this. Player-infested trees behave identically to naturally spawned ones.
If a Piko loses its home tree and has already picked up an item, it will seek out and eat Seeds Food on the ground. This is the only situation that will make Piko eat voluntarily, which may be a design error.
An infested tree always spawns the same color Piko each day, unless it's piko is killed or captured, in which case the color of the next piko is random.

It is common when you chop a Tea Tree that Pikos may spawn or race in from nearby trees and steal one of your Logs, Twigs, or Seed Pods. In addition, an attack from a Piko or Orange Piko will, like frogs, cause the item in the leftmost slot of the inventory bar to drop from the player's inventory. Be very careful when chopping Tea Trees at night with a Lantern on the ground. Pikos might steal it, and without any other light sources you will be completely vulnerable to Charlie.
Player can infest a tree with Pikos by releasing any captured piko nearby and letting it climb the tree.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Piko là một Mob trung lập độc quyền trong DLC ​​​​Don't Starve: Hamlet. Ngoài ra còn có một biến thể màu cam hiếm hơn được gọi là Orange Piko.

Perhaps a consequence of its proximity to tea trees? |wigfrid = I hath nö use för it. |webber = I wonder if he likes tea parties. |walani = Oh, I get it. |warly = I prefer English Breakfast. |woodlegs = Aye, 'tis a useful brewed. |wilba = OF MOST EXCELLENT FANCY TAILS |wormwood = Fluffy Nabber |wheeler = Has a strangely caffeinated smell. |choose = webber |source = examining an Orange Piko}} The '''Piko''' is a neutral [[Mob]] exclusive to the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet]]'' DLC. There is also a rarer orange variant called the '''Orange Piko'''. Both Piko and Orange Piko spawn from [[Tea Tree]]s, and seem more likely to spawn near clumps of several trees growing close together. Each Piko has a "home tea tree". They will only climb this tree, unless they are caught in a [[trap]] and released. They will retreat to their home tree at dusk, when frightened, or after picking up an item. They will not leave the tree until next dawn or the player relogs. They do not seem to spawn from tea trees planted by the player, unless one is caught in a trap and put on the ground near that tree. After a few seconds, the Piko will run up the nearest tree, "infesting" it with Piko. Chopping down an infested tree returns some of the items stolen by it's Piko. However, there is a bug which causes the infested [[Tea Tree|tree]]'s stump to still spawn and harbor Pikos. Digging up the stump solves this. Player-infested trees behave identically to naturally spawned ones. If a Piko loses its home tree and has already picked up an item, it will seek out and eat [[Food Type#Seeds|Seeds Food]] on the ground. This is the only situation that will make Piko eat voluntarily, which may be a design error. An infested tree always spawns the same color Piko each day, unless it's piko is killed or captured, in which case the color of the next piko is random. If an item is dropped on the ground (either [[Food|edible]] or not) near a Piko or Orange Piko, there is a chance that it will stock it in its cheeks and flee with it. It is common when you chop a [[Tea Tree]] that Pikos may spawn or race in from nearby trees and steal one of your [[Logs]], [[Twigs]], or [[Seed Pod]]s. Take caution not to drop stacks of an item, as one Piko is able to steal it all. Pikos will run from the player, unless they are trying to steal an item on the ground or there is a [[Moon Cycle|full moon]]/[[aporkalypse]] in progress. During a [[Full Moon]] or [[Aporkalypse]], Pikos will glow, their eyes turn red and they become aggressive. In addition, an attack from a Piko or Orange Piko will, like frogs, cause the item in the leftmost slot of the inventory bar to drop from the player's inventory. After said item is on the ground, the Piko/Orange Piko prioritizes storing the item in its cheeks and retreating to its home [[Tea Tree]]. Pikos can be caught using a [[Trap]]. During a [[Moon Cycle|Full Moon]], carrying a captured Piko will cause the player to emit a reddish glow, similar to the effects of eating a [[Glow Berry]]. Piko will return to their home at dusk and on the night of the new moon. They leave their homes at the new moon and return immediately. =={{Pic32|Icon Tools}} Usage== {{Recipe |item1 = Orange Piko |count1 = 2 |item2 = Sweetener |item3 = Ice |pot = yes |result = Iced Tea }} {{Recipe |item1 = Orange Piko |count1 = 2 |item2 = Sweetener |item3 = Filler |pot = yes |result = Tea }} == {{pic|32|Prototype}} Tips== * Be very careful when chopping [[Tea Tree]]s at night with a [[Lantern]] on the ground. Pikos might steal it, and without any other light sources you will be completely vulnerable to [[Charlie]]. * Player can infest a tree with Pikos by releasing any captured piko nearby and letting it climb the tree. * Using an item to bait a Piko is an easy way to kill them, as they prioritize retrieving the item and running away with it rather than running from players when approached. Players can get free hits on Pikos as they run toward the item. If the Piko picked up the item, the player can retrieve it by killing the Piko, as they run toward a given Tea Tree in a predictable straight line. ** Pikos cannot pick up backpacks, but will relentlessly try to anyways, making for very effective bait. * Interestingly, [[Abigail]] hates Pikos and will attack them if they are too near. An easy way to farm them for morsels. * Pikos can be used to obtain drops from pig guards without other nearby guards aggroing the player. If a piko picks up a guard drop then is killed it will drop what it picked up and the guards will no longer identify it as belonging to a guard and the player can pick it up without getting attacked. =={{Pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs== * Pikos can hide in Tree stumps as if the tree was still present and will thus run up an invisible tree and disappear. * If dropped in a shop, game can crash. * if it tries to get a wet sunken object, you hitting it makes it walk on water. once it is on water, it will try to get back to its tea tree, but you have to hit it again for it to get back on land. you cannot use this to retrieve wet sunken objects as if you do it will stay as a wet sunken object, but on land. * If you kill a Piko just before it gets trapped it provides you with a morsel and a trapped Piko. * If you kill or capture an aggressive Piko on a full moon, another non-aggressive Piko will immediately spawn from it's tree. == {{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * The use of [[Orange Piko]]s in making Tea and Iced Tea is a pun on [[Wikipedia:Tea leaf grading#Orange_pekoe|orange pekoe tea]]. * The Piko may be loosely based off of the real-life [[Wikipedia:Pika|pika]], who are known to steal food from its own kind, and [[Wikipedia:Pica_(disorder)|pica]], an eating disorder characterized by the consumption of inedible items. =={{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery== Piko Sleeping.png|A sleeping Piko.
